Oracc: SAAo/SAA05 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part II: Letters from the Northern and Northeastern Provinces

SAA 05 129. Where are the King’s Logs? (CT 53 872)[via saao/saa05]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

⸢nu-uk 01 me 20 GIŠ.ÙR⸣-MEŠ

(1) I (wrote him): "Where are the king's 120 logs?" He returned and told me: "I have brought in the third (installment of) logs, and l[ef]t it on the border of Quruba."

o 2'2'

ša LUGAL a-le-e

o 3'3'

šu-ú i-su-ḫur iq-ṭi-bi-a

o 4'4'

ma-a GIŠ.ÙR-MEŠ šal-ši-u-te

o 5'5'

ú-se-ri-ba ina ta-ḫu-me

o 6'6'

ša KUR.qu-ru-ba ⸢ur-ta⸣-me

o 7'7'

KUR.⸢ú⸣-[x x x x x x]

(7) The U[kkean ......]

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

⸢x⸣+[x x x x x x x x x]

r 2'2'

i-⸢su⸣-[x x] ⸢x⸣+[x x x x x]

(r 2) He re[turned ...] and said to the Ukkean: "Why do you bring the king's troops to me? I will have them removed." When the troops departed, the Ukkean [said to m]e:

r 3'3'

a-na KUR.ú-ka-a.a iq-ṭí-⸢bi⸣

r 4'4'

ma-a e-mu-qi ša LUGAL a-na mì-ni

r 5'5'

ina UGU-ḫi-ia tú-ba-⸢la⸣

r 6'6'

a-na-ku-ma a-ba*-ak

r 7'7'

ki-i e-mu-qi ú-na-me-šá-ni

r 8'8'

KUR.ú-ka-a.a [iq-ṭi]-⸢bi⸣-a

r 9'9'

[ma-a x x x x x x]-⸢x-ka⸣

(r 9) "[... yo]ur [......]

rest broken away

(Break)

Edge
e. 1e. 1

[x x x]+⸢x⸣ GIŠ.ÙR-MEŠ KUR.né-ru-bu ⸢x⸣+[x x x x]

(e. 1) [......] the logs (at) the pass [......]

e. 22

[x x x] a-na LÚv.A—KIN ša ma-da-⸢a⸣ [x x x x]

(e. 2) [......] to the messenger of Adâ [......]

e. 33

[x x x] ⸢KUR⸣ URU.pa-ie-e LÚv.ERIM-⸢MEŠ⸣ [x x x x]

(e. 3) [......] of the town Paye the men [......]

e. 44

[x x x]           ⸢URU⸣.[x x x x]

(e. 4) [......] the ci[ty ......]

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Giovanni B. Lanfranchi and Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part II: Letters from the Northern and Northeastern Provinces (State Archives of Assyria, 5), 1990. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314281/.
